rationale.R1.2: The most natural reading of 'check this' on a technical system paper is to verify internal logical coherence; the artifact contains at least one direct self-contradiction — Section 3 states high-difficulty queries (s ≥ τ) go to Tier-2, yet the cost paragraph says the 70B model is reserved for queries the classifier 'marks as easy,' inverting the stated policy — making logical consistency a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: Verifying that reported numbers support their interpretive claims is a primary reading of 'check this'; the artifact asserts 587 ms p99 tail latency under FROST is 'a substantial improvement in tail responsiveness' relative to a 412 ms baseline, but 587 > 412 is a ~42% regression, not an improvement — making evidence-claim alignment central and potentially load-bearing.
